title: helianthemum guttatum var. milleri
intro: Helianthemum guttatum var. milleri, also known as rockrose, is a flowering plant species in the family Cistaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, ulcers, and skin conditions due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of rockrose include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiseptic properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Rockrose is often prepared as a salve, oil, or infusion, and can be used topically or taken internally in moderation under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
